chunks.text:
# CustomerMaster E-Commerce System - Business Glossary

## Document Overview
**System**: CustomerMaster v3.2.1 (Legacy)
**Last Updated**: March 2015
**Purpose**: Business terminology definitions for data migration project
**Target System**: New ERP Platform (Migration Q4 2015)

---

## Core Business Entities

### Customer
**Definition**: An individual or organization that has purchased products or services from the company. Customers are stored in the `tblCustomer` table with legacy identifiers ... [TRUNCATED]

## Domain-Specific Terminology

### Backorder
**Definition**: A situation where a customer orders a product that is out of stock. The order is accepted, but the line item is marked as BACKORDERED until inventory arrives.

**Business Impact**: Backorders are allowed even with negative stock quantities. This is intentional to support pre-orders and special orders.

---

### Fulfillment
**Definition**: The process of picking, packing, and shipping orders to customers. Managed by the warehouse team.... [TRUNCATED]

## Legacy System Quirks

### Hungarian Notation
The legacy system uses Hungarian notation prefixes:
- `str` = string (VARCHAR)
- `int` = integer (INT)
- `lng` = long integer (INT)
- `flt` = float/money (DECIMAL)
- `bol` = boolean (BIT)
- `dtm` = datetime (DATETIME)

**Migration Note**: These prefixes are inconsistent. For example, `intCustID` is actually a VARCHAR, and `lngOrderID` is a regular INT.

### Table Naming Inconsistency
Different parts of the system use different naming conventions:
-... [TRUNCATED]

## Migration Priority Guidelines

### Critical Path (Must Migrate First)
1. **tblCustomer** - Required for all orders
2. **tblProduct** - Required for line items and inventory
3. **vw_SalesOrderHdr** - Core transactional data
4. **ord_line_item** - Order details

### Secondary (Can Migrate in Parallel)
5. **inv_txn_log** - Inventory audit trail
6. **tblPayment** - Payment records
7. **tblOrderStatusHistory** - Order status audit

### Low Priority (Migrate Last)
8. **tblShippingCarrier** - Refere... [TRUNCATED]

strCategory (VARCHAR 100)
    - Product category
    - Hierarchical: "Clothing > Shirts > Casual"

strBrand (VARCHAR 100)
    - Brand or manufacturer name
    - Free text field

dtmIntroduced (DATETIME)
    - Product launch date
    - When product was added to catalog

dtmDiscontinued (DATETIME)
    - Discontinuation date
    - NULL if currently active

Legacy Deprecated Fields:
------------------------
prod_num (VARCHAR 20)
    - OLD: Legacy product number
    - Superseded by strSKU
    - Retai... [TRUNCATED]

qty (INT)
    - Quantity ordered
    - Must be positive integer

amt (DECIMAL 10,2)
    - Line item total amount (qty * unit_price)
    - At time of order (snapshotted, not current price)

disc_amt (DECIMAL 10,2)
    - Discount amount applied to this line
    - Can be zero

line_status (VARCHAR 20)
    - Line item status
    - Values: "PENDING", "PICKED", "SHIPPED", "BACKORDERED"

product_code (VARCHAR 50)
    - REDUNDANT: Copy of tblProduct.strSKU
    - Denormalized for reporting performance
  ... [TRUNCATED]

Email (VARCHAR 150)
    - User email address
    - For notifications

LastLogin (DATETIME)
    - Last successful login timestamp
    - NULL for new users

CreatedDate (DATETIME)
    - Account creation date
    - DEFAULT GETDATE()

================================================================================
TABLE: tblOrderStatusHistory
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Audit log of order status changes
Naming Convention: Verbose (tbl pre... [TRUNCATED]

CardNumberText (VARCHAR 100)
    - SECURITY ISSUE: Full card number stored as text!
    - Should be tokenized
    - Contains unencrypted PAN (Primary Account Number)

CardHolderName (VARCHAR 100)
    - Cardholder name
    - As provided by customer

AuthorizationCode (VARCHAR 50)
    - Payment gateway auth code
    - Proof of successful authorization

================================================================================
MIGRATION NOTES:
=================================================... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.object: A record of stock quantity changes for audit purposes

triplets.object: Stores customer master data from the legacy CRM system

entities_post_resolution.name: `Ord Line Item`
entities_post_resolution.definition: An order (sales order) is a formal request from a customer for products or services, encompassing order details, status, line items, payments, and associated data within a business transaction system.
synonyms: A commercial transaction request from a customer for one or more products
A record of stock quantity changes for audit purposes
A sellable item in the company's catalog
An individual or organization that has purchased products or services from the company
An individual product within an order, specifying quantity and price
Full card number stored as text
The new system will use integer customer IDs (cust_id) instead of alphanumeric strCustID codes
Updated by trigger on any field change
When product was added to catalog
both active and discontinued products with the same SKU pattern
independent status (can be backordered while others ship)
optional but strongly recommended for order notifications
quantity × unit price at time of order
the price at time of sale
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
The `ord_line_item` table contains redundant copies of product data (`product_code`, `item_name`) for historical accuracy. | PaymentStatus (VARCHAR 20)
    - Payment processing status
    - Values: "PENDING", "COMPLETED", "FAILED", "REFUNDED" | TrackingURL (VARCHAR 500)
    - URL template for tracking
    - Example: "https://www.ups.com/track?tracknum={TRACKING_NUM}" | **Definition**: A commercial transaction request from a customer for one or more products. | **Definition**: A record of stock quantity changes for audit purposes. | **Definition**: A sellable item in the company's catalog. | CreatedDate (DATETIME)
    - Account creation date | bolActive (BIT)
    - Active carrier flag | - Amount is calculated as quantity × unit price at time of order | Amount (DECIMAL 12,2)
    - Payment amount | Amount (DECIMAL 12,2)
    - Payment amount
    - Must match order total | **Definition**: An individual or organization that has purchased products or services from the company. | **Definition*

entities_post_resolution.name: Parcel Service Ups Fed
entities_post_resolution.definition: These are major parcel delivery carriers used for shipping and logistics services.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
CarrierCode (VARCHAR 20, UNIQUE)
    - Carrier code for API integration
    - Examples: "UPS", "FDX", "USPS" | CarrierName (VARCHAR 100)
    - Carrier display name
    - Examples: "UPS", "FedEx", "USPS"

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Active Status Flags
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute indicating whether a customer or product is currently active, available, or in circulation within the business system.
synonyms: 1 = available for sale, 0 = discontinued or out of circulation
Inactive carriers are not available for new orders
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
bolActive (BIT)
    - Active customer flag
    - 1 = active, 0 = inactive | bolActive (BIT)
    - Product availability flag
    - 1 = available for sale
    - 0 = discontinued or out of circulation | bolActive (BIT)
    - Active carrier flag
    - Inactive carriers not available for new orders | fltPrice (DECIMAL 10,2)
    - Unit selling price
    - Must be > 0
    - Stored with 2 decimal places | bolActive (BIT)
    - Active carrier flag | - Some orders reference customers marked as inactive | - Customers can be marked as inactive (bolActive = 0) but are never deleted

entities_post_resolution.definition: A specific date in the Gregorian calendar, used to mark a particular point in time.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Last Updated: 2015-08-14 | **Last Updated**: March 2015

entities_post_resolution.name: Category Color Size
entities_post_resolution.definition: Category-Color-Size is a product attribute format used in SKU identifiers to specify product details such as category, color, and size.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
strSKU (VARCHAR 50, UNIQUE)
    - Stock Keeping Unit
    - Format: Category-Color-Size (e.g., "SHIRT-BLU-L")
    - Must be unique | - SKUs follow the format: Category-Color-Size (e.g., SHIRT-BLU-L) | strCategory (VARCHAR 100)
    - Product category
    - Hierarchical: "Clothing > Shirts > Casual"

entities_post_resolution.name: Data Migration Notes
entities_post_resolution.definition: A collection of notes or annotations that document specific instructions, issues, or considerations related to data migration processes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Data Migration Notes**: The new system will use integer customer IDs (cust_id) instead of alphanumeric strCustID codes. | The `lngOrderID` uses long integer format. | The `ord_line_item` table contains redundant copies of product data (`product_code`, `item_name`) for historical accuracy. | txtNotes (TEXT)
    - Free-form notes about customer
    - Used by customer service team
    - Contains migration notes, special handling

entities_post_resolution.name: Frequently Queried Fields
entities_post_resolution.definition: A free text field is a data entry component that allows users to input unstructured textual information within a database or form.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- No indexes on frequently queried fields (customer email, order date range) | strBrand (VARCHAR 100)
    - Brand or manufacturer name
    - Free text field | - TEXT fields used for variable-length data (should use VARCHAR(MAX)) | Customer's full name (free text field) | - SECURITY ISSUE: Full card number stored as text!

entities_post_resolution.name: No Indexes
entities_post_resolution.definition: This entity refers to either naming conventions without prefixes or the absence of indexes on database fields, depending on context.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - No indexes on frequently queried fields (customer email, order date range) | - No prefix: Group, User (reserved words!)

entities_post_resolution.name: Varchar
entities_post_resolution.definition: A VARCHAR is a variable-length character string data type used in databases to store text data with a specified maximum length.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - `tblProduct.unit_cost` is VARCHAR(20) instead of DECIMAL | - `tblCustomer.strCustID` is VARCHAR(50) instead of INT | for example, `intCustID` is actually a VARCHAR

entities_post_resolution.name: Inv Txn Log
entities_post_resolution.definition: The inv_txn_log is a database table that records inventory transaction activities, including user interactions.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - `inv_` prefix: inv_txn_log | 5. **inv_txn_log** - Inventory audit trail | Stored in `inv_txn_log` table. | - No foreign key constraint on `inv_txn_log.user_id`

tables_enriched.name: Customer
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about customers, including their identifiers, contact details, geographic location, account status, and notes.

tables_enriched.name: Product Table
tables_enriched.description: This table stores detailed information about products, including identifiers, descriptions, pricing, stock levels, and categorization data for inventory management and sales analysis.

tables_enriched.name: View Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: This view contains header information for sales orders, including order details, dates, status, financial totals, and shipping information.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: This table stores detailed information about each individual product line within customer orders, including quantities, pricing, discounts, and status.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory Transaction Log
tables_enriched.description: This table records all inventory transactions, including product movements, quantities, transaction types, and associated user and reference information.

tables_enriched.name: Group
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about different groups within the organization, including their hierarchy and status.

tables_enriched.name: User
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about users, including their login credentials, roles, and account activity timestamps.

tables_enriched.name: Order Status History
tables_enriched.description: This table records the history of status changes for customer orders, including who made the change and when.

tables_enriched.name: Table Shipping Carrier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about shipping carriers, including their identifiers, names, codes, tracking URLs, and whether their bill of lading process is active.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about payments made for customer orders, including payment methods, dates, amounts, statuses, and card details.
